en: And it happened that while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul passed through the inland country and came to Ephesus. There he found some disciples.
zh: 亞波羅在哥林多的時候，保羅經過內地一帶，來到以弗所。他遇見幾個門徒，

en: And he said to them, "Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?" And they said, "No, we have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit."
zh: 問他們說：“你們信的時候，受了聖靈沒有？”他們說：“沒有，連聖靈賜下來這件事，我們也沒有聽過。”

en: And he said, "Into what then were you baptized?" They said, "Into John's baptism."
zh: 保羅問：“那麼你們受的是甚麼洗呢？”他們說：“是約翰的洗禮。”

en: And Paul said, "John baptized with the baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the one who was to come after him, that is, Jesus."
zh: 保羅說：“約翰施的是悔改的洗禮，他告訴人民當信在他以後要來的那一位，就是耶穌。”

en: On hearing this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
zh: 他們聽見了，就受洗歸入主耶穌的名下。

en: And when Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in tongues and prophesying.
zh: 保羅為他們按手，聖靈就降在他們身上，他們就用各種的語言講話，並且說預言。

en: There were about twelve men in all.
zh: 他們一共約有十二人。

en: And he entered the synagogue and for three months spoke boldly, reasoning and persuading them about the kingdom of God.
zh: 一連三個月，保羅都到會堂裡去，放膽宣講，辯論 神的國的事，勸導人。

en: But when some became stubborn and continued in unbelief, speaking evil of the Way before the congregation, he withdrew from them and took the disciples with him, reasoning daily in the hall of Tyrannus.
zh: 可是有些人心裡剛硬，不受勸化，在群眾面前毀謗這道，保羅就離開他們，也叫門徒與他們分開。他每天在推喇奴學院跟人辯論。

en: This continued for two years, so that all the residents of Asia heard the word of the Lord, both Jews and Greeks.
zh: 這樣過了兩年，全亞西亞的居民，無論猶太人或希臘人，都聽見了主的道。

en: And God was doing extraordinary miracles by the hands of Paul,
zh: 神藉著保羅的手，行了一些不平凡的神蹟。

en: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons that had touched his skin were carried away to the sick, and their diseases left them and the evil spirits came out of them.
zh: 甚至有人把保羅貼身的手巾圍巾拿去，放在病人身上，病就好了，邪靈也出來了。

en: Then some of the itinerant Jewish exorcists undertook to invoke the name of the Lord Jesus over those who had evil spirits, saying, "I adjure you by the Jesus, whom Paul proclaims."
zh: 那時，有幾個趕鬼的猶太人，周遊各處，擅自用主耶穌的名，向身上有邪靈的人說：“我奉保羅所傳的耶穌的名，命令你們出來。”

en: Seven sons of a Jewish high priest named Sceva were doing this.
zh: 有一個猶太人士基瓦，是祭司長，他的七個兒子都作這事。

en: But the evil spirit answered them, "Jesus I know, and Paul I recognize, but who are you?"
zh: 邪靈回答他們：“耶穌我認識，保羅我也知道；你們是誰？”

en: And the man in whom was the evil spirit leaped on them, mastered all of them and overpowered them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
zh: 邪靈所附的那人就撲到他們身上，制伏了兩人，勝過了他們，使他們赤著身帶著傷，從那房子逃了出來。

en: And this became known to all the residents of Ephesus, both Jews and Greeks. And fear fell upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was extolled.
zh: 所有住在以弗所的猶太人和希臘人，都知道這件事；大家都懼怕，尊主耶穌的名為大。

en: Also many of those who were now believers came, confessing and divulging their practices.
zh: 也有許多信了的人，來承認和述說自己行過的事。

en: And a number of those who had practiced magic arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all. And they counted the value of them and found it came to fifty thousand pieces of silver.
zh: 又有許多行巫術的人，把他們的書堆在一起當眾燒掉。他們估計書價，才知道共值五萬塊銀子。

en: So the word of the Lord continued to increase and prevail mightily.
zh: 這樣，主的道大有能力地興旺起來，而且得勝。

en: Now after these events Paul resolved in the Spirit to pass through Macedonia and Achaia and go to Jerusalem, saying, "After I have been there, I must also see Rome."
zh: 這些事以後，保羅心裡定意要經過馬其頓、亞該亞，往耶路撒冷去。他說：“我到了那邊以後，也該去羅馬看看。”

en: And having sent into Macedonia two of his helpers, Timothy and Erastus, he himself stayed in Asia for a while.
zh: 於是從服事他的人中，派了提摩太和以拉都兩人去馬其頓，自己暫時留在亞西亞。

en: About that time there arose no little disturbance concerning the Way.
zh: 那時，因這道起了大擾亂，

en: For a man named Demetrius, a silversmith, who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen.
zh: 有一個銀匠，名叫低米丟，是製造亞底米女神銀龕的。他讓技工們作了不少的生意。

en: These he gathered together, with the workmen in similar trades, and said, "Men, you know that from this business we have our wealth.
zh: 他把這些人和同業的工人聚集起來，說：“各位，你們知道，我們是靠這生意賺錢的。

en: And you see and hear that not only in Ephesus but in almost all of Asia this Paul has persuaded and turned away a great many people, saying that gods made with hands are not gods.
zh: 現在你們看見了，也聽見了，這個保羅不單在以弗所，而且幾乎在整個亞西亞，說服了，也帶壞了許多人，說：‘人手所做的，都不是神。’

en: And there is danger not only that this trade of ours may come into disrepute but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis may be counted as nothing, and that she may even be deposed from her magnificence, she whom all Asia and the world worship."
zh: 這樣，不只我們這一行要給人鄙視，就是大女神亞底米的廟也會給人撇棄，連全亞西亞和普天下所敬拜的女神也要垮臺，威榮盡都失掉了。”

en: When they heard this they were enraged and were crying out, "Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!"
zh: 他們聽了，怒氣沖沖喊著說：“以弗所人的女神，偉大的亞底米啊！”

en: So the city was filled with the confusion, and they rushed together into the theater, dragging with them Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians who were Paul's companions in travel.
zh: 全城騷動起來，他們捉住了保羅的旅伴馬其頓人該猶和亞里達古，齊心衝進了劇場。

en: But when Paul wished to go in among the crowd, the disciples would not let him.
zh: 保羅想要到人群當中去，門徒卻不許。

en: And even some of the Asiarchs, who were friends of his, sent to him and were urging him not to venture into the theater.
zh: 還有幾位亞西亞的首長，是保羅的朋友，派人來勸他，不要冒險到劇場裡去。

en: Now some cried out one thing, some another, for the assembly was in confusion, and most of them did not know why they had come together.
zh: 那時大家叫這個喊那個，亂成一團，大多數的人都不知道聚集的原因。

en: Some of the crowd prompted Alexander, whom the Jews had put forward. And Alexander, motioning with his hand, wanted to make a defense to the crowd.
zh: 猶太人把亞歷山大推到前面，群眾中有人把這事的因由告訴他。亞歷山大作了一個手勢，要向民眾申辯。

en: But when they recognized that he was a Jew, for about two hours they all cried out with one voice, "Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!"
zh: 大家一認出他是猶太人，就異口同聲高呼：“以弗所人的女神，偉大的亞底米啊！”喊了約有兩個鐘頭。

en: And when the town clerk had quieted the crowd, he said, "Men of Ephesus, who is there who does not know that the city of the Ephesians is temple keeper of the great Artemis, and of the sacred stone that fell from the sky?
zh: 後來，書記官安撫群眾說：“以弗所人哪！誰不知道你們的城，是看守大亞底米的廟，又是看守宙斯那裡降下的神像的呢？

en: Seeing then that these things cannot be denied, you ought to be quiet and do nothing rash.
zh: 這些事既然是駁不倒的，你們就應當平心靜氣，不可輕舉妄動。

en: For you have brought these men here who are neither sacrilegious nor blasphemers of our goddess.
zh: 你們帶來的這些人，既沒有行劫廟宇，也沒有褻瀆我們的女神。

en: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen with him have a complaint against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls. Let them bring charges against one another.
zh: 如果低米丟和同他一起的技工要控告誰，大可以告上法庭，或呈交總督；讓他們彼此控告好了。

en: But if you seek anything further, it shall be settled in the regular assembly.
zh: 如果還有其他的事件，可以在合法的集會裡，謀求解決。

en: For we really are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since there is no cause that we can give to justify this commotion."
zh: 今天的動亂，本來是無緣無故的，我們可能有被控告的危險；關於這次的騷動，我們實在無法解釋。”

en: And when he had said these things, he dismissed the assembly.
zh: 說了這些話，就把群眾解散了。
